'''Niado Duegad''' was a native of<ref name="CCG" /> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Vodran CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|310|Vodran}}</ref> [[Vodran/Legends|Vodran]]. His<ref name="CCG" /> [[Unidentified dianoga-like species|species]] was similar to the [[Dianoga/Legends|dianoga]],<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|213|Duegad, Niado}}</ref> a creature native to Duegad's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]].<ref name="Vodran CSWE" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a conflict between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], Duegad served as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]].<ref name="CCG" /> His [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="CSWE" /> and culture controlled their environment through [[Terraforming/Legends|terraforming]] abilities. Niado Duegad's enhanced [[adrenal gland]]s allowed for short bursts of incredible strength. Duegad had reddish-brown skin with black stripes and a single hazel-[[Color/Legends|colored]] [[Eye/Legends|eye]]. He also possessed a level of [[Force-attunement]].<ref name="CCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Niado Duegad's origins date back to the production stages of [[1983]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], when several [[Alien/Legends|alien]] maquettes were created for possible inclusion in the scenes taking place in [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|desert palace]]. The concept of a dianoga-like alien known as "Alien 18" was among those scrapped before the film's final release. Being non-[[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], the "Alien 18" model was too difficult to realize as a puppet.<ref name="Chronicles">''[[Star Wars Chronicles]]''</ref>

The alien was first named as Niado Duegad on a card showing a modified image of the maquette for the [[1998]] ''[[Special Edition Limited]]'' expansion set of [[Decipher]]'s [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]], thus bringing the maquette into [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[canon]].<ref name="CCG" /> Rearranged, Niado Duegad's name is an anagram of "Dianoga Dude."<ref name="Article">{{SW|url=news/back-from-the-drawing-board-recycled-star-wars-aliens|text=Back from the Drawing Board: Recycled ''Star Wars'' Aliens|int=Back from the Drawing Board|nolive=1}}</ref>
